q01.name: Rival Warband
q01.desc: A rival clan's raiders cut the herd trails. The warchief pays for them driven off or dead.

q02.name: The Dragon's Tithe
q02.desc: A whole valley pays it tribute and calls that peace. The crown calls it rebellion with wings.

q03.name: The Giant of the Border Marches
q03.desc: Border forts flattened, garrisons walking home weaponless. The marshal wants the marches quiet again.

q04.name: The Troll Under the Pass
q04.desc: The mountain road pays tribute in carts and drivers. The clans want the pass opened.

q05.name: The Proving Hunt
q05.desc: The clan sets a beast worth a name. Bring back its hide and the clan pays in iron and standing.

q06.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q06.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.

q07.name: Spiders in the Undercity
q07.desc: The vent shafts are webbed shut and the air below is going bad. Small pay, small heroes welcome.

q08.name: The Engine Gone Feral
q08.desc: They built it too big and fed it too well, and now the lower works belong to it.

q09.name: The Engine Gone Feral
q09.desc: They built it too big and fed it too well, and now the lower works belong to it.

q10.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q10.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.

q11.name: The Restless Crypt
q11.desc: The churchyard dead are not staying put. The parish purse opens for whoever settles them.

q12.name: Deserters Turned Raiders
q12.desc: Soldiers who kept their steel and lost their oaths, living off the villages they once guarded.

q13.name: Wolves at the Folds
q13.desc: Sheep gone, then a shepherd. The commons want the pack dug out of the hills.

q14.name: The Grudge War
q14.desc: A rival clan pressed an old grudge with new axes. The ledger wants balancing.

q15.name: The Breaker in the Dark
q15.desc: Something big moved into the worked seams and it does not share. The mine masters want their tunnels back.

q16.name: Things in the Deep Roads
q16.desc: The under-way to the second hold is silked and skittering. The toll-guild pays by the cleared mile.

q17.name: The Breaker in the Dark
q17.desc: Something big moved into the worked seams and it does not share. The mine masters want their tunnels back.

q18.name: Deserters Turned Raiders
q18.desc: Soldiers who kept their steel and lost their oaths, living off the villages they once guarded.

q19.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q19.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.

q20.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.

q21.name: The Engine Gone Feral
q21.desc: They built it too big and fed it too well, and now the lower works belong to it.

q22.name: The Proving Hunt
q22.desc: The clan sets a beast worth a name. Bring back its hide and the clan pays in iron and standing.

q23.name: Scrap-Hounds Loose in the Works
q23.desc: The bosses' hound-pack slipped its chains and dens in the gearworks. Pay docked for every eaten shift-worker.

q24.name: The Restless Crypt
q24.desc: The churchyard dead are not staying put. The parish purse opens for whoever settles them.

q25.name: The Flooded Hold
q25.desc: An old hold, lost and lately dry again. What was buried with it did not stay buried.

q26.name: The Grudge War
q26.desc: A rival clan pressed an old grudge with new axes. The ledger wants balancing.

q27.desc: The vent shafts are webbed shut and the air below is going bad. Small pay, small heroes welcome.

q36.name: Wyvern on the Crags
q36.desc: A shadow over the herds, and herders who point at the high crags and will not climb.
